subject: Science

unit.unitOverview: This unit is about pushes and pulls. A push moves a thing away from you. A pull brings a thing toward you. Pushes and pulls are all around us every day. We push a swing. We pull a toy cart. We roll a ball. We open a door. In this unit the child will play, watch, and talk about how things move.
learningPath: Preview the goals and core ideas
Learn methods and study worked examples
Complete guided practice, experiments and games
Apply, explain and complete the Unit Challenge

provenance.transformation: Structured directly from the Stage 1 science workbook source documents for screen presentation.

outcomes: Recognise a push and a pull as forces.
Make an object move by pushing or pulling it.
Make a moving object slow down or stop.
Change how fast an object moves.
Change the direction in which an object moves.
Observe and describe pushes and pulls in play and at home.

concepts.title: Pushes
concepts.explanation: This unit is about pushes and pulls. A push moves a thing away from you. A pull brings a thing toward you. Pushes and pulls are all around us every day. We push a swing. We pull a toy cart. We roll a ball. We open a door. In this unit the child will play, watch, and talk about how things move.
concepts.example: You are the teacher or the parent. The child is 5 or 6 years old and is only just beginning to read. So you read aloud, and the child plays and answers. Warmth, games, and gentle talk matter more than writing. Keep each…

concepts.title: Pulls
concepts.explanation: You are the teacher or the parent. The child is 5 or 6 years old and is only just beginning to read. So you read aloud, and the child plays and answers. Warmth, games, and gentle talk matter more than writing. Keep each lesson short and happy. Praise every try. Let the child touch and move real things, because that is how young children learn best.
concepts.example: The whole unit takes about four to five weeks. Do a little each day. Ten to twenty minutes of play and talk is plenty for one sitting. There is no need to finish a whole lesson in one go. If the child is happy and busy,…

concepts.title: Making Things Move
concepts.explanation: The whole unit takes about four to five weeks. Do a little each day. Ten to twenty minutes of play and talk is plenty for one sitting. There is no need to finish a whole lesson in one go. If the child is happy and busy, keep going. If the child is tired, stop and come back another day. Learning that feels like play is the best learning of all.
concepts.example: This guide gives you the words to say, the games to play, and the questions to ask. You do not need to be a science teacher. You only need to play with your child, watch what happens, and talk about it together. Every…

concepts.title: Making Things Stop
concepts.explanation: This guide gives you the words to say, the games to play, and the questions to ask. You do not need to be a science teacher. You only need to play with your child, watch what happens, and talk about it together. Every time you push a swing, roll a ball, or pull a door, you are teaching real science.
concepts.example: Did You Know? A push and a pull are both called a force. A force is just a word for a push or a pull. Nothing moves, stops, or turns without a push or a pull!

concepts.title: Fast and Slow
concepts.explanation: Did You Know? A push and a pull are both called a force. A force is just a word for a push or a pull. Nothing moves, stops, or turns without a push or a pull!
concepts.example: Ask Your AI Tutor Ask your AI tutor for a short, simple rhyme about pushing and pulling. Ask for three more easy words that mean push or pull. Ask for a story about Amina pushing a swing for her little brother.

activities.title: Push a Toy Car
steps: Follow the investigation plan in your experiments book.

steps: Follow the investigation plan in your experiments book.

steps: Follow the investigation plan in your experiments book.

activities.title: Make a Ball Stop
activities.materials: Notebook, pencil and safe things you can find at home
steps: Look for an example of making things stop around your home or outside.
Watch it closely for a few minutes and note three things you notice.
Write or draw what you observed, using the unit's science words.
Explain your finding to a family member or your teacher.

activities.title: Push Hard, Push Gently
activities.materials: Notebook, pencil and safe things you can find at home
steps: Look for an example of fast and slow around your home or outside.
Find examples at home and sort them into groups, then explain your rule.
Write or draw what you observed, using the unit's science words.
Explain your finding to a family member or your teacher.

activities.title: Change the Way It Goes
activities.materials: Notebook, pencil and safe things you can find at home
steps: Look for an example of changing direction around your home or outside.
Change one thing, keep everything else the same, and record what happens.
Write or draw what you observed, using the unit's science words.
Explain your finding to a family member or your teacher.

rules.title: Key idea 1
rules.text: , the child is very young. Keep the lessons full of play, smiles, and real things to touch. Do not rush. If the child is tired, stop and try again tomorrow. Every push of a swing and every pull of a toy cart is a science lesson. You are doing wonderful work by playing and talking with your child. Well done!

terms: Move a thing away from you.
Go from one place to another.
To change which way you go.
To move by turning over and over.
To move flat along the ground.

vocabulary.meaning: Move a thing away from you.
vocabulary.example: Every time you push a swing, roll a ball, or pull a door, you are teaching real science.
Every time you push a swing, roll a ball, or pull a door, you are teaching real science.
A push and a pull are both called a force.

vocabulary.meaning: Go from one place to another.
vocabulary.example: In this unit the child will play, watch, and talk about how things move.
If the child is tired, stop and come back another day.
A big, strong force makes a thing move fast.
A small, gentle force makes a thing move slow.

vocabulary.meaning: To change which way you go.
vocabulary.example: See that a push or a pull can make a thing move, stop, or turn.

vocabulary.meaning: To move by turning over and over.
vocabulary.example: Every time you push a swing, roll a ball, or pull a door, you are teaching real science.

vocabulary.meaning: To move flat along the ground.
vocabulary.example: A wooden block, a shoe, and a spoon (things that slide)

reasoningPrompts.prompt: Explain the key idea in Making Things Stop.
reasoningPrompts.modelAnswer: This guide gives you the words to say, the games to play, and the questions to ask. You do not need to be a science teacher. You only need to play with your child, watch what happens, and talk about it together. Every time you push a swing, roll a ball, or pull a door, you are teaching real science.

questions.question: Opening a door by pulling it is a...
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Pulling the door towards you is a pull.

questions.question: Kicking a ball away from you is a...
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Kicking pushes the ball away, so it is a push.

questions.question: A push or a pull is called a...
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: A push or a pull is a force.

questions.question: To make a moving toy stop, you...
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: A force such as a push can make a moving thing stop.

questions.question: If you push a swing harder, it goes...
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: A bigger push makes things move faster.

questions.question: A push on the side of a ball can...
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: A force can change the direction a thing moves in.

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: Moving softly, not fast.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Slow means Moving softly, not fast..

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: To change which way you go.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Turn means To change which way you go..

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: To move by turning over and over.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Roll means To move by turning over and over..

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: To move flat along the ground.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Slide means To move flat along the ground..

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: Move a thing away from you.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Push means Move a thing away from you..

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: Bring a thing toward you.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Pull means Bring a thing toward you..

rounds.clue: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
rounds.explanation: Pulling the door towards you is a pull.

rounds.prompt: To make a moving toy stop, you...
rounds.clue: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
rounds.explanation: A force such as a push can make a moving thing stop.

rounds.prompt: Which science word matches this meaning: Moving softly, not fast.?
rounds.clue: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
rounds.explanation: Slow means Moving softly, not fast..

games.title: Mastery Mix: Push
games.description: Practise push through four short challenges.

rounds.prompt: Which science word matches this meaning: Move a thing away from you.?
rounds.clue: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
rounds.explanation: Push means Move a thing away from you..

rounds.prompt: Kicking a ball away from you is a...
rounds.clue: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
rounds.explanation: Kicking pushes the ball away, so it is a push.

rounds.prompt: If you push a swing harder, it goes...
rounds.clue: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
rounds.explanation: A bigger push makes things move faster.

rounds.prompt: Which science word matches this meaning: To change which way you go.?
rounds.clue: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
rounds.explanation: Turn means To change which way you go..

games.title: Unit Champion: Pull
games.description: Practise pull through four short challenges.

rounds.prompt: Which science word matches this meaning: Bring a thing toward you.?
rounds.clue: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
rounds.explanation: Pull means Bring a thing toward you..

rounds.prompt: A push or a pull is called a...
rounds.clue: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
rounds.explanation: A push or a pull is a force.

rounds.prompt: A push on the side of a ball can...
rounds.clue: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
rounds.explanation: A force can change the direction a thing moves in.

rounds.prompt: Which science word matches this meaning: To move by turning over and over.?
rounds.clue: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
rounds.explanation: Roll means To move by turning over and over..

selfAssessment: I can recognise a push and a pull as forces.
I can make an object move by pushing or pulling it.
I can make a moving object slow down or stop.
I can change how fast an object moves.
I can change the direction in which an object moves.
I can observe and describe pushes and pulls in play and at home.
